title: Validate, Sanitize, Escape Review
prompt: Review a write path that accepts a title, URL, HTML description, and numeric display order. Explain what to validate, what to sanitize before storage, and what to escape at output.
hints: Validate shape and permission first; sanitize for storage; escape late for the specific output context.

model_answer.summary: The write path should validate intent and shape, sanitize stored values, and escape late for each output context.
model_answer.work: Before accepting the write, check capability with current_user_can or a REST permission_callback and verify the nonce for form submissions. Validate that title is present, URL is a valid URL, description only allows intended HTML, and display order is numeric. Sanitize title with sanitize_text_field, URL with esc_url_raw for storage, description with wp_kses_post or a stricter allowed-tags list, and display order with absint. At output time, escape late by context: esc_html for text, esc_url for links, esc_attr for attributes, and wp_kses_post only for intended HTML output.
checks: Test missing or invalid fields fail validation before storage.
Verify nonce, capability, permission_callback, or current_user_can protects the write path.
Confirm sanitize_text_field, esc_url_raw, wp_kses_post, and absint are used for storage.
Review output context and escape late with esc_html, esc_url, esc_attr, or HTML-safe output.
Add tests for title, URL, HTML description, and display order edge cases.
model_answer.why_this_is_good: It keeps the three safety phases distinct, which helps LLMs avoid the common mistake of treating sanitization as output security.
common_misses: Escaping before storage instead of sanitizing for storage.
Using one function for every field.
Forgetting the permission or nonce check.
Ignoring attribute, HTML, and URL output context differences.
model_answer.revision_prompt: Revise by splitting validate, sanitize, and escape into separate phases with WordPress APIs and a capability, nonce, permission_callback, or current_user_can check.
